reforms.description: The Greek government introduced a new programme in 1998 called 'Quality for the Citizen', aimed at improving public administration services through the creation of one-stop shops.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving public services can enhance efficiency and reduce transaction costs for citizens and businesses, potentially fostering a more conducive environment for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has led to the creation of one-stop shops for public services.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to modernize public services, which is crucial for improving overall administrative efficiency.
reforms.source_quote: The government introduced a new programme in 1998, called 'Quality for the Citizen', which aims at improving the services provided by the public administration.

reforms.description: In 1999, Greece adopted a Code of Administrative Procedures to streamline administrative processes and enhance client-oriented services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining administrative procedures can reduce compliance costs and improve service delivery, fostering a better environment for business and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Two new codes were adopted, including a Code of Administrative Procedures.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the efficiency of public administration, which is essential for improving the business environment.
reforms.source_quote: In 1999, two new codes were adopted: a Code of Administrative Procedures, to streamline administrative procedures and introduce client-oriented services.

reforms.description: The government plans to enhance public support for R&D and innovation through horizontal programmes, addressing the lag in public sector financing for R&D compared to other OECD countries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public support for R&D is expected to stimulate innovation and enhance competitiveness in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has submitted proposals to enhance support for R&D and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards prioritizing R&D funding, which is crucial for long-term economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Support to R&D and innovation should become a key concern of horizontal programmes, as Greece is lagging behind the other OECD countries.

reforms.description: The Greek government plans to allocate GRD150 billion of privatization revenues to co-finance the startup of firms specializing in new technologies, including IT and mobile telecommunications.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship in high-tech sectors, contributing to economic growth and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to spend GRD150 billion on the scheme.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in the startup ecosystem, aiming to foster innovation in emerging technology sectors, which is crucial for Greece's economic development.
reforms.source_quote: Over the next four years, the government plans to spend GRD150 billion of privatization revenues on a scheme to co-finance, with private sector venture capital, the start-up of firms which specialise in new technologies.
